blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: ‘At thy root lives Brahma, in the stem lives Vishnu, and at the top lives Shiva, I bow unto thee, O ashvath.’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: Flowers and fruits of sorts should be offered to the sacred tree. The next prayer is : ‘O ashvath, thou, the abode of Agni, the God of Fire, and the asylum of Vishnu, I bow unto thee.’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: ‘Then the 108 perambulations should be performed. The offerings should consist of pearls, gold, diamonds, and other jewels, copper and brass, and pots full of foodstuffs. All the white coins and other offerings placed before the tree should then be handed over to the Brahman preceptor. In order to please Somā a married Brahman woman should be worshipped under the tree. Brahmans should be fed well. Finally, the devotee should take her meal observing silence all the while.’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: ‘O king ! Ask Draupadi, Subhadrā, and Uttarā to observe this vrata. It will result in the fœtus of Uttarā regaining life.’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: But, out of consideration for the poor, Dharma enquired—how people without means could perform such a costly puja ?

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: Bhishma said : ‘By offering fruits, flowers, food, clothes and whatever one can afford.’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: ‘O king !’ entreated Bhishma, ‘do perform the vrata as quickly as possible !’

blocks.label: Text
blocks.html: The dhobi is undoubtedly classed with Chamārs, Nats, (Doms), Baruds, Kaivartas, Meds, and Bhils. That a woman of this caste, even if pious, was worshipped by a high-class Brahman woman, shows the elevation of the depressed classes under certain conditions.
